problem_solved: Provides integrated electronic patient record (EPR) systems and care management software for healthcare providers, enabling digital transformation across NHS trusts and social care organizations including hospice and palliative care settings.
who_buys_it: NHS trusts, hospitals, community care providers, local authorities, social care organizations
key_features: CareFlow EPR - cloud-based electronic patient record platform
Adult Social Care case management system
Integrated medicines management and prescribing
why_varden_should_care: Proven model of integrating healthcare IT across multiple care settings; replicable approach to connecting hospitals, community care, and social services that could be adapted to other markets.

problem_solved: Provides a single platform for patients to access, manage, and share their health information with healthcare professionals, family, and carers - specifically including end-of-life care coordination and planning.
who_buys_it: NHS trusts, clinical commissioning groups, hospitals, GP practices, health boards
key_features: Personal health record with care plan management
Secure messaging with healthcare professionals
End-of-life care coordination and sharing capabilities
business_model: SaaS (free for patients, paid by healthcare organizations)
why_varden_should_care: Pioneering patient-controlled health records model with proven NHS integration; the end-of-life care focus demonstrates a replicable approach to sensitive healthcare coordination that empowers patients and families.

problem_solved: Digitizes homecare operations to help care providers deliver better quality care, reduce administrative burden, and improve outcomes for people receiving care at home - applicable to palliative home care.
who_buys_it: Homecare agencies, domiciliary care providers, care organizations
key_features: Unified care management platform with digital care plans and eMAR
Automated rostering and workforce management
Real-time monitoring and CQC compliance benchmarking (Q-Score)
why_varden_should_care: Fast-growing homecare technology platform with 1000+ care businesses; demonstrates how to build an all-in-one solution for care coordination that could be adapted for hospice-at-home and palliative care services.

problem_solved: Enables remote patient monitoring and virtual ward services, allowing patients to be monitored safely at home - increasingly relevant for palliative and end-of-life care where patients prefer to remain at home.
who_buys_it: NHS trusts, clinical commissioning groups, GP practices, community health providers
key_features: Virtual ward platform for home-based care monitoring
Remote patient monitoring for respiratory, cardiovascular, and long-term conditions
Integration with NHS systems and clinical workflows
why_varden_should_care: Leading UK remote monitoring platform with proven virtual ward deployments; the model of enabling care at home through technology is directly applicable to hospice and palliative care services.

problem_solved: Provides population health management and shared care records that connect health and social care data, enabling proactive identification and support of patients including those with palliative care needs.
who_buys_it: NHS organizations, integrated care systems, local authorities, health boards
key_features: CIPHA - Combined Intelligence for Population Health Action platform
Shared care records connecting multiple providers
Remote patient monitoring and virtual wards
why_varden_should_care: 30+ years of experience in UK population health; demonstrates how to build integrated care platforms that could identify and coordinate care for palliative patients across health and social care boundaries.

problem_solved: Provides person-centred care management software for social care providers, enabling better care coordination, documentation, and outcomes for people in care homes and home care settings.
who_buys_it: Care homes, homecare providers, supported living organizations, adult social care providers
key_features: Digital care planning and recording with person-centred approach
Rostering and workforce management
Integration capabilities with NHS and health systems
why_varden_should_care: First accredited by PRSB as Quality Partner; demonstrates how to build care management software that bridges social care and health systems - relevant for hospice care that often spans both sectors.

problem_solved: Provides clinical IT systems used by 100,000+ healthcare professionals across primary care, community care, pharmacy, and A&E - including systems used by hospice and palliative care providers.
who_buys_it: GP practices, community health providers, pharmacies, A&E departments, hospices
key_features: EMIS Web - integrated clinical system for primary and community care
Patient information sharing across care settings
Prescribing and medicines management
why_varden_should_care: #1 clinical system in UK primary care; demonstrates the scale achievable in healthcare IT and how clinical systems can be extended to community and hospice settings.

problem_solved: Provides comprehensive care business management software for care organizations of scale, supporting residential, domiciliary, supported living, and complex care provision.
who_buys_it: Large care providers, local authorities, housing associations, independent care organizations
key_features: Unified care management across multiple care types and locations
Integrated rostering, payroll, and invoicing
Single source of truth for multi-site care organizations
why_varden_should_care: Demonstrates how to build scalable care management software that handles complexity across multiple care settings - relevant for hospice organizations that may operate inpatient, community, and home care services.
